mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly depressing S&P 500 valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds +0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of high-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand, forcing equity markets to price in lower near-term earnings and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a Rank IC below -0.02, or positive returns occur in >55% of co-declining months.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a deceleration in the index of industrial production reflects capacity constraints and slowing output growth, which triggers profit margin compression and negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: In late-cycle regimes, declining INDPRO is followed by positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the mechanism shows no predictive power outside of late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profits and prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as markets reprice risk.
failure_condition: High inflation months are followed by positive 5-day returns in >65% of observations, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust economic activity and rising household incomes, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Months with strong payroll growth yield negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the Rank IC for the signal remains below 0.02 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects strong aggregate demand and healthy economic growth, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive equity market performance over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth quarters are followed by negative 60-day returns in >50% of instances, or the hypothesis fails to achieve a statistically significant positive Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth outpaces inflation, it signals efficient capacity utilization and expanding profit margins, which supports equity valuations and produces positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The condition of INDPRO outpacing CPIAUCSL is followed by negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when tested against single-series baselines.
